title: Leakage-resistant high-cardinality strategy encodings
summary: `Driver`, `Race`, and their interactions are high-cardinality and strategy-heavy.
rationale: `Driver`, `Race`, and their interactions are high-cardinality and strategy-heavy. Public evidence favors regularized target encoding over crude frequency/integer encoding, and CatBoost’s ordered categorical processing is specifically designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage.
implementation_hint: Test either CatBoost on mostly raw categoricals plus compact numeric features, or explicit fold-local regularized target encodings for `Driver`, `Driver_Compound`, `Race_Compound_Stint`, `Year_Race`, and `Driver_Race`. Use chronological ordering inside each train fold if possible, and compare against the current frequency features.
expected_effect: Captures persistent driver/team/race style differences that simple frequency counts miss, without exploding sparse one-hot interactions.
risk: Naive target encoding will overfit badly; must be race-blocked and OOF, with strong shrinkage for rare categories.
